claim: Gentse Feesten 2026 runs 17–26 July with free music, street theatre and parades across the historic centre
quote: The Ghent Festivities (Gentse Feesten) take place from 17 to 26 July 2026. Cultural popular festival with numerous performances of various national and international artists.

claim: Gentse Feesten draws about 2 million visitors and is recognised as Flemish intangible heritage — one of Europe's largest popular festivals
quote: The Ghent Festivities are among the biggest popular festivals in Europe and have been recognised as Flemish intangible heritage. About 2 million visitors attend the festival every year.

claim: Patershol Festivities run 14–16 August 2026 and Bijloke Wonderland 20–30 August; Film Fest Gent 7–18 October; Gent Jazz 3–18 July
quote: Patershol festivities 14–16 August 2026. Bijloke Wonderland 20–30 August 2026. Film Fest Gent 7–18 October 2026. Gent Jazz 3–18 July 2026.

claim: Floraliën Ghent 2026 runs 1–10 May at the Floraliënhal, Kuipke and Botanical Garden — Ghent's flagship flower festival held every five years
quote: Floraliën Gent 1 May to 10 May 2026. The next edition organises the four-yearly flower and plant festival in Ghent and other floral and green events throughout Flanders.

claim: Gent Smaakt is a free five-day culinary festival across the historic centre, 13–17 May 2026 (Ascension weekend)
quote: Gent Smaakt: Culinair festival, 13 mei - 17 mei 2026. About 20 local restaurateurs and caterers serve their best specialties. Access free of charge.

claim: Polé Polé runs alongside Gentse Feesten 17–26 July with Latin/Afro/global music on Ghent's waterways
quote: Polé Polé Festival — Gentse Feesten 2026. Latin, Afro, and global rhythms with tropical cocktails on Ghent's iconic waterways.

claim: Lotto Zesdaagse Vlaanderen-Gent (Six Days of Ghent track cycling) runs 17–22 November 2026 at 't Kuipke
quote: Lotto Zesdaagse Vlaanderen-Gent, 17 to 22 November 2026, 't Kuipke.

claim: Ghent Christmas Market: 3 December 2026 – 3 January 2027, ~150 chalets across the centre with a 44 m Ferris wheel and ice rink
quote: Ghent's Christmas market runs from 3rd December 2026 to 3rd January 2027. More than 150 wooden chalets spread across the historic city center; 44-meter Ferris wheel, ice skating rink, Winter Wonder Castle at Gravensteen.

claim: Ghent Light Festival is triennial — no 2026 edition; next is 2027
quote: The Ghent Light Festival takes place every three years. No edition is planned for 2026.

claim: Bruges Procession of the Holy Blood: Thursday 14 May 2026, 14:30–17:30, route from Dijver to Burg
quote: The Procession of the Holy Blood will take place on Thursday, May 14, 2026, starting at 2:30 PM from the Dijver. The procession concludes at 5:30 PM with the closing ceremony at the Burg.

claim: The Procession of the Holy Blood is on UNESCO's Representative List of Intangible Cultural Heritage; ~1,700 participants and 30,000–45,000 spectators each year
quote: Procession of the Holy Blood in Bruges, inscribed in 2009 on the Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity.

claim: Cactusfestival Bruges 2026: 10–12 July at Minnewaterpark — alternative pop and rock
quote: 43rd Cactusfestival 2026 in Bruges, Belgium — Open-Air Concerts July 10–12 at Minnewaterpark.

claim: Bruges Beer Festival 18th edition: 12–13 September 2026 at the Bruges Meeting and Convention Centre
quote: Bruges Beer Festival 2026, 12 and 13 September 2026, Bruges Meeting and Convention Centre (BMCC).

claim: Bruges Winter Glow runs 20 November 2026 – 14 February 2027, with two centre markets at Markt and Simon Stevinplein
quote: Winter Glow festival runs from 20 November 2026 to 14 February 2027. On the Markt traditional Christmas market; on Simon Stevinplein short chain and craft products.

claim: Binche Carnival is on UNESCO's Representative List of Intangible Cultural Heritage; iconic Gilles characters and orange-throwing tradition
quote: Carnival of Binche, inscribed in 2008 on the Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity. Three days preceding Lent host carnival festivities that mobilize the historic centre.

claim: Binche Carnival 2026 takes place 15–17 February (Sunday through Shrove Tuesday)
quote: The Carnival of Binche in Belgium is celebrated from February 15-17, 2026.

claim: Aalst Carnival is a 3-day pre-Lent street parade with satirical floats; concludes Tuesday with 'Voil Jeanetten' (men in drag) and the burning of the puppet on Grote Markt
quote: Aalst Carnival is a 3 day street carnival where local carnival groups show their humorous vision on local and global topics in a parade. Tuesday: 'Voil Jeanetten'.

claim: Aalst Carnival is inscribed on UNESCO's Representative List of Intangible Cultural Heritage
quote: Aalst carnival, inscribed in 2010 on the Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity.

claim: Brussels Flower Carpet 2026: 13–16 August on the Grand Place, ~70 × 24 m of begonias laid by ~120 volunteers; 2026 honours Japan
quote: Flower Carpet 2026, 13 to 16 August 2026. Carpet of 70 m x 24 m composed by about 120 volunteers, with nearly 1 million flowers in less than 4 hours. For this 24th edition, Japan will be honoured on the Grand-Place.

claim: Tomorrowland 2026 takes place across two weekends in Boom: 17–19 and 24–26 July
quote: The event will run over two weekends at its spiritual home of Boom, from 17th to 19th and 24th to 26th July.

claim: Couleur Café 2026: 26–28 June at Osseghem Park (next to the Atomium), Brussels — global/world music
quote: Couleur Café 2026 takes place from June 26 to 28 at Osseghem Park near Brussels' Atomium, celebrating global music and culture.

claim: Pukkelpop 2026: 20–23 August in Hasselt — Belgium's flagship rock/indie/electronic four-dayer
quote: Pukkelpop 2026 in Hasselt, 20–23 August 2026.

claim: Rock Werchter 2026: 2–5 July at Werchter — Belgium's biggest rock festival
quote: Rock Werchter 2 to 5 July 2026. Cactus Festival 10-12 July in Bruges. Couleur Café 26-28 June. Live is Live festival June 27-29 in Antwerp.

claim: Keukenhof Gardens 2026: 19 March – 10 May, 7 million bulbs / 800 tulip varieties; peak bloom mid-April – early May
quote: Keukenhof 2026 runs from 19 March to 10 May. With 7 million bulbs planted each year across 800 tulip varieties.

claim: Braderie de Lille 2026: 5–6 September — Europe's largest flea market, 2–3 million visitors, ~100 km of stalls, free entry
quote: Braderie de Lille 2026: Saturday 5 and Sunday 6 September. Largest and oldest flea market in Europe; nearly 100 kilometers of stalls; 2 to 3 million visitors. More than 500 tons of mussels consumed each year.

claim: Magisch Maastricht Vrijthof: Christmas market on the Vrijthof with skating/curling rink, carousel and 45 m enclosed-gondola Ferris wheel
quote: Magisch Maastricht Vrijthof. Skating rink, curling rink, carousel, and a 45-meter-high Ferris wheel with enclosed glass gondolas overlooking the Vrijthof.

claim: Cologne Cathedral Christmas Market: 25 November – 23 December 2026, 150+ stalls beside the cathedral, 3-min walk from the central station
quote: The Cologne Cathedral Christmas Market runs from Nov 25 - Dec 23, 2026 and features over 150 stalls. 3 minute walk from the central train station.

claim: Stavelot Laetare carnival is held mid-Lent (fourth Sunday of Lent) — known for its white-hooded 'Blancs Moussis' characters
quote: Stavelot, Capitale du Laetare et ses Blancs Moussis (Carnaval Wallon). Laetare Sunday — the fourth Sunday of Lent.

claim: Zomer van Antwerpen ('Summer of Antwerp') runs across the city from late June to August with free/low-cost theatre, circus and open-air cinema
quote: Zomer van Antwerpen transforms the city from June to August at Eilandje and Het Zuid: theatre performances, circus acts, and open-air cinema. Many events free or low-cost.
